Cupressus grandifolia is a type of evergreen tree in the cypress family (Cupressaceae), known for its distinctive needle-like foliage and conical shape. The health benefits of Cupressus grandifolia are largely attributed to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cupressus grandifolia include terpenes, such as borneol and bornyl acetate, as well as essential oils like cypressene and alpha-pinene. In traditional medicine, Cupressus grandifolia is often used to make herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
